pattern: Popping the highest-priority active event with a smallest-id tiebreak is a priority-queue job; because a priority change or a poll can strand old queue entries, the queue is checked against a live priority map so stale entries are simply discarded when they surface.
hints: Scanning every active event to find the current best on each pollHighest call is too slow if there are many events — keep candidates ordered so the best one surfaces without a full rescan.

updatePriority changing an event's priority would normally mean finding and moving an entry buried inside the queue — instead just push a fresh entry for the new priority and let the old one become obsolete.

Keep a heap ordered by (priority descending, eventId ascending) alongside a map of each event's current true priority; pollHighest pops heap entries, skipping any whose priority no longer matches the map, until it finds one that does — that one is real and gets removed from the map too.

constraints.note: each row [eventId, priority]; all eventIds distinct, 1<=eventId,priority<=1000000000

edge_inputs.description: sample 1: a tie between two events is broken by the smaller id, then a priority update changes who wins next
sample 2: repeated polling eventually empties the manager
a three-way tie on priority is broken by the smallest eventId among all of them
raising a low-priority event above the current leader changes who pollHighest returns next
polling an already-emptied manager keeps returning -1
a single event with eventId and priority at the constraint extremes
updating the same event's priority more than once keeps only the most recent value
a manager that starts with several events polls them off strictly in priority order

stress.description: random updatePriority/pollHighest traffic seeded with a batch of distinct events, restocking the pool as it drains
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    next_id = [1]
    def fresh_id():
        v = next_id[0]
        next_id[0] += 1
        return v
    seed_events = []
    active = {}
    n0 = rng.randint(1, 30)
    for _ in range(n0):
        eid = fresh_id()
        pr = rng.randint(1, 500)
        seed_events.append([eid, pr])
        active[eid] = pr
    ops = ['EventManager']
    args = [[seed_events]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.55 and active:
            eid = rng.choice(list(active.keys()))
            pr = rng.randint(1, 500)
            ops.append('updatePriority')
            args.append([eid, pr])
            active[eid] = pr
        else:
            ops.append('pollHighest')
            args.append([])
            if active:
                best_id = max(active.items(), key=lambda kv: (kv[1], -kv[0]))[0]
                del active[best_id]
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: generator's own active map only tracks ids it has itself created, so updatePriority always targets a still-active event as the problem guarantees
